title: Authentication and Security
description: Managing credentials for repositories and auditing packages for vulnerabilities

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Runs CLI commands, responds to interactive auth prompts

actors.name: RepositoryServer
actors.description: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or generic HTTP repository returning 401/403

actors.name: SystemEnvironment
actors.description: Provides proxy env vars (http_proxy, https_proxy, no_proxy) read by ProxyManager

actors.name: SecurityAdvisorySource
actors.description: Remote advisory repositories queried during package audit

use_cases.name: Audit Packages for Vulnerabilities
use_cases.description: Developer runs `composer audit` to check installed/locked packages against security advisories
steps: Developer invokes audit command (with optional flags)
AuditCommand::getPackages() resolves package list (installed or from lock file)
AuditConfig::fromConfig() loads audit configuration (ignoreList, severities, abandoned policy)
Auditor::audit() queries RepositorySet for advisories and reports results
options: --locked: use composer.lock instead of installed packages
use_cases.returns: exit code (min 255), non-zero on vulnerabilities found

use_cases.name: Authenticate with Repository
use_cases.entry_point: promptAuthIfNeeded(url, origin, statusCode, reason, headers, retryCount, responseBody): array{retry, storeAuth}
use_cases.description: When an HTTP 401/403 occurs, AuthHelper prompts for or retrieves credentials and retries the request
sub_flows: GitHub: authorizeOAuth() or authorizeOAuthInteractively(); handles rate-limit and SSO headers
GitLab: authorizeOAuth() or authorizeOAuthInteractively(); rejects invalid private/ci tokens
Bitbucket: requestToken() to exchange credentials for OAuth token; authorizeOAuthInteractively() if needed
Generic HTTP: asks username/password interactively; throws TransportException if non-interactive
use_cases.result: returns {retry: true, storeAuth: bool|'prompt'} or throws TransportException

use_cases.name: Store Credentials
use_cases.description: After a successful authenticated request, optionally persist credentials to auth.json
steps: If storeAuth===true: immediately write to auth config source
If storeAuth==='prompt': ask user 'Do you want to store credentials for X?'

use_cases.name: Add Authentication Headers to Request
use_cases.description: Inject the correct auth header type into outgoing HTTP request options
auth_strategies: SSL client certificate: sets ssl options from auth.json
Custom headers: injects arbitrary headers from auth.json
use_cases.note: api.bitbucket.org and api.github.com are recursively redirected to their root origins

use_cases.name: Configure Proxy for HTTP Requests
use_cases.description: ProxyManager reads proxy environment variables and returns the appropriate proxy for each request URL
steps: getProxyForRequest(url): returns RequestProxy.none(), RequestProxy.noProxy(), or proxy.toRequestProxy(scheme)
use_cases.security_note: http_proxy from $_SERVER is only read in CLI (PHP_SAPI=cli|phpdbg) to prevent header injection in CGI

use_cases.name: Throw Security Exception on Invalid Signature
use_cases.description: Thrown by repository code when a package signature is broken or missing
use_cases.note: Extends \Exception directly. Caught by callers to abort insecure downloads.

relationships: UC1 <<include>> UC5 (HTTP requests through proxy during advisory fetch)
UC1 <<extend>> UC2 (advisory fetch may trigger 401 → auth flow)
UC2 <<include>> UC4 (inject auth headers on retry)

key_classes.description: Marker exception for broken/missing package signatures

important_notes: AuditCommand supports exit codes for CI use: non-zero = vulnerabilities found
AuthHelper supports 6 auth strategies: Bearer, GitHub OAuth, GitLab OAuth/private-token, Bitbucket OAuth, SSL cert, HTTP Basic
ProxyManager is a singleton; reset() is provided for tests
ProxyManager restricts http_proxy to CLI SAPI only (security: CGI could allow header injection)
RepositorySecurityException is a thin marker class — logic lives in repository implementations
AuthHelper::addAuthenticationHeader() is deprecated since Composer 2.9; use addAuthenticationOptions()
